news.headline: Brookline Capital Markets Initiates Coverage of Perimeter Medical Imaging AI with Buy Rating
news.summary: Brookline Capital Markets analyst Kemp Doliver started coverage of Perimeter (TSXV:PINK; OTCQX:PYNKF) at Buy with a C$1.54 (US$1.11) price target, noting Claire's FDA Breakthrough Device status should speed CMS review of the company's planned New Technology APC filing for Medicare outpatient reimbursement, with a decision expected by year-end. Shares closed at C$0.32 on July 13, well below the new target.

news.headline: Paradigm Capital Names Perimeter Medical Imaging a New Healthcare Top Pick
news.summary: Paradigm Capital analyst Scott McAuley named Perimeter (TSXV:PINK; OTCQX:PYNKF) a new Top Pick with a speculative buy rating, raising his price target to C$0.95 from C$0.70 based on 9x projected 2027 revenue and recent M&A comparables in breast-cancer surgery devices. Shares closed at C$0.275 on July 3, well below the new target.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI to Participate in the Planet MicroCap Showcase in Las Vegas
news.summary: Perimeter announced CEO Adrian Mendes would present a company update on commercial strategy and upcoming milestones at the Planet MicroCap Showcase on June 17, 2026, with a live and archived webcast available to investors.

news.headline: Perimeter Selected for INOVAIT Pilot Fund to Advance AI for Breast Cancer Surgery
news.summary: Perimeter was selected for a Government of Canada-backed INOVAIT Pilot Fund contribution of up to roughly C$148,000, plus a C$100,000 Mitacs award, to advance next-generation AI capabilities for its FDA-approved Claire breast-cancer margin assessment device.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI Closes Final Tranche of Non-Brokered Private Placement
news.summary: Perimeter closed the second and final CDN$3.28 million tranche of its non-brokered convertible debenture private placement, bringing the total raise to CDN$6.04 million, with major shareholder Social Capital and CEO Adrian Mendes participating.

news.headline: Perimeter Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Perimeter reported Q1 2026 recurring revenue of about $385,000, a 30% cut in operating expenses to $3.2 million, a 31% narrower net loss of $3.0 million, and a 41% reduction in cash used in operations to $2.2 million, reflecting early commercial traction following the March 2026 FDA approval of Claire, including its first two commercial Claire placements at top-tier hospitals.

catalysts.when: H2 2026 (decision expected by year-end 2026; effective date targeted Jan 1, 2027, possibly Oct 1, 2026)
catalysts.label: CMS Medicare reimbursement decision for Claire
catalysts.detail: Management is filing a New Technology APC application via the MEARIS quarterly process under Medicare's Outpatient Prospective Payment System, with Claire's FDA Breakthrough Device status expected to speed CMS review; CEO Adrian Mendes said on the Q1 2026 call the application was being submitted to CMS with hope of reimbursement in place by early 2027. A positive decision would likely be followed by commercial payer policy reviews (UnitedHealth, Anthem, Cigna, Aetna) in Q1-Q2 2027.

catalysts.when: Late August-early September 2026 (estimated, unconfirmed by company)
catalysts.label: Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Next scheduled quarterly earnings release covering Claire's early U.S. commercial rollout post-FDA approval; third-party data providers' estimates for the date have ranged from around August 25 to as late as September 1, 2026, but as of July 23, 2026 Perimeter has still not issued an official press release scheduling the call (it typically announces the date about a week ahead, as it did for Q1 2026).

catalysts.label: Potential Nasdaq uplisting
catalysts.detail: Management has publicly discussed a possible move from OTC/TSX-V to Nasdaq to broaden the capital markets pool for the stock, but no formal application or timeline has been confirmed.

catalysts.label: Additional health-system adoption agreements for S-Series OCT / Claire
catalysts.detail: Following the systemwide Intermountain Health agreement and FDA PMA for Claire, and the first two commercial Claire placements disclosed with Q1 2026 results, Perimeter is pursuing further hospital-system rollout deals to expand recurring procedure revenue.

pulse: Since Claire's landmark March 2026 FDA PMA approval as the first AI-enabled intraoperative breast-cancer margin device, Perimeter has stacked supportive developments: non-dilutive INOVAIT/Mitacs funding, a closed C$6.04M convertible debenture financing, and back-to-back bullish analyst calls in early-to-mid July from Paradigm Capital (Top Pick, C$0.95 target) and Brookline Capital Markets (new Buy initiation, C$1.54 target), with shares trading around C$0.30-0.32. As of July 24, 2026, a fresh round of searches across press-release wires, IR channels, and analyst coverage again found no new company developments since the July 14 Brookline initiation report -- newsflow has now been quiet for over a week and a half. The key re-rating catalyst ahead remains the CMS Medicare New Technology APC reimbursement decision for Claire, expected by year-end 2026 with a targeted January 1, 2027 effective date, alongside continued hospital-system rollout, Q2 2026 earnings tentatively estimated for late August to early September 2026 (still not yet officially scheduled by the company), and a still-unconfirmed potential Nasdaq uplisting.
